target_mode: Mode 4 — Proposals & Self-Fixes (and possibly a new Mode 5 — Cross-Agent Complaint Audit)
title: Cross-agent complaint audit — tinker should read what every other agent writes
summary: The core failure mode revealed by two recent incidents (human-notes status-vocabulary drift + workspace-sync skip-log noise) is not two separate bugs. It is one structural gap: tinker is not systematically reading the output files that other agents produce. Every agent surfaces its complaints, blockers, stale items, and silent-skips in plain-text or structured fields — but no agent walks those outputs looking for patterns that persist across runs. This directive subsumes DIRECTIVE-20260422-001; handle both instances under one coherent proposal.

incident_1_human_notes.observed: Operator wrote HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-PHASE-A-CLARIFICATION-001 and -002 with status="unclaimed". Analyst preflight (analyst.md line ~105) triggers on status==="pending" only. Notes were silently ignored until operator noticed by chance. See also decider/social/curmudgeon human-notes — probably same latent bug.
incident_1_human_notes.surface: Silent. No agent complained. Operator had to flip the bit manually.

incident_2_sync_skip_log.observed: 13 files in monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/ accumulating monotonically-growing skips with reason "mtime-guard; git newer". Root cause: decider.md dual-write loop was calling new Date().toISOString() per-base, so workspace and clone stamps drifted by 2-5 ms. Bug was latent for ~4 weeks. Fixed today in commit d948d6a.
incident_2_sync_skip_log.surface: Skip-log grew from 5 to 15 entries per sync run over 8 consecutive runs. Nobody was reading the skip-log. Tinker cited FUSE-drift in his reports but framed it as git-owned-file-freshness (leading to PROP-010) rather than drilling into per-file drift patterns.

context.shared_pattern: An agent wrote a complaint to a file. No other agent reads that file. Complaint persisted for weeks before a human noticed. This is the exact silent-failure mode we flag in the dome review — we should not replicate it in our own pipeline.

proposed_fix_spec.deliverable: A single PROP proposing (a) an immediate one-time audit and (b) a recurring audit pattern. Specific choices are yours; optimize for "every agent-surfaced complaint that persists across N runs becomes a tinker finding, loudly."
audit_surfaces_to_consider: monitor/integrity/workspace-sync-skips.jsonl — group by reason+path, flag paths skipping >N runs in a row
monitor/decisions/daily-report-*.json — decider daily report (error sections, unresolved items)
extraction_rules_to_consider: Severity fields: scan every report_*.json for .severity in [critical,major] and .status in [open,pending,unresolved]
Human-notes status enum: walk all four human-notes.json files and enumerate distinct status values. If any non-canonical (non {pending,consumed,deferred,rejected}) value appears, flag
Append-only integrity: walk monitor/*/{reviews,expansions,proposals,drafts}/ — any mtime-drift between workspace and clone for matching IDs is a red flag (content diff, not just mtime)

Persistence detection: if the same complaint-key (file path + reason, or ID + severity) appears in two consecutive tinker audits without resolution, upgrade it to a finding. Three consecutive = urgent finding.
thinking_prompts: Which audit surfaces are already covered by existing tinker modes? Do not re-implement what Mode 1 already does.
Which complaint-keywords produce signal vs. noise? Tune thresholds against actual recent output.
Where should findings land? New monitor/tinker/complaint-audit-*.json? Appended to daily report? Both?
How to avoid token cost blowup — the full text of every agent artifact per run would be expensive. Suggest reading only latest-summary.txt equivalents and diffing structured-field summaries.
Persistence detection requires cross-run memory. Where is that stored? monitor/tinker/complaint-state.json? Merge with existing report format?

deliverable_requested.format: PROP file in monitor/tinker/proposals/ following existing PROP-* naming conventions.
contents: One-time audit: enumerate today's distinct complaint-keys across ALL audit surfaces listed above. Group by (agent, severity, keyword). Report counts and point-of-origin.

Human-notes status-vocabulary section: enumerate every distinct status value found across all four human-notes.json files with counts per agent. Recommended canonical enum + enforcement mechanism (schema validator, widened preflight, default-pending, or hybrid). Cross-reference incident_1.
Skip-log audit section: audit workspace-sync-skips.jsonl for the last 30 days. Group by (path-prefix, reason). Any prefix with >20 skips in a rolling 7-day window is a candidate finding.
Recurring audit design: propose when tinker runs this (every tinker run? new Mode 5? sub-step of Mode 1?), which files get scanned each pass, persistence thresholds.
Concrete prompt patch: exact text changes to tinker.md.

Escalation path: how do findings from this audit become actionable? Auto-filed as a monitor/tinker/operator-directives/ directive to self? Appended to daily report? Cross-posted to decider human-notes?
Cost estimate: expected token add per tinker run vs. current cost.
Rollback plan if audit produces false positives at a rate that drowns out real signal.

non_goals: Do NOT propose replacing any existing agent output surface. The files mentioned above are correctly scoped; this directive is purely about READING them.
Do NOT propose that tinker autonomously fix anything it finds. Findings are surfaced to the operator and/or the relevant agent via established channels.
Do NOT add a ninth scheduled agent. This is a tinker scope expansion, not a new agent.
Do NOT attempt to unify or merge the four human-notes.json files. Per-agent separation is deliberate.

related_commits: c2a50b3 — HNOTE-SEC2B1-FORMULA-DRIFT-001 (social draft stranded, no ISS filed)
d948d6a — decider pop-write timestamp drift fix + 13-file workspace-clone cleanup

completion_criteria: Tinker produces a PROP file addressing the deliverable list. Operator reviews + approves the recurring-audit design and persistence thresholds before the tinker.md patch is applied through the normal clone-side edit -> build.js publish flow.
